fixes.reason: remove duplicate Supplementary Table S18 citation
fixes.old: These analyses provide cellular and pathway plausibility, not paired validation or cell-cell interaction validation (Supplementary Table S18).
fixes.new: These analyses provide cellular and pathway plausibility, not paired validation or cell-cell interaction validation.

fixes.old: Table 3. Continuous-spectrum, randomization and clustering-stability strategy.
fixes.new: Table 3. Broad-cell composition-adjusted computational sensitivity summary.

main_figures.context: Here we investigated whether public RNA and cytometry data could support a bounded, compartment-aware model of sepsis-associated immunoparalysis. The study architecture and claim boundaries are summarized in Figure 1. Ou

Across seven independent public bulk transcriptomic cohorts, inflammatory and immunometabolic programs were consistently directionally increased in sepsis, whereas MHC-II/CD74, HLA-DR, and adaptive/T-cell context program

Among 499 complete sepsis cases, PCA identified a dominant continuous immune-decoupling gradient explaining 55.9% of case-only signature variance. This gradient was positively aligned with the six-gene host-response pane

Cytometry summaries were interpreted with explicit artifact boundaries. Frequency and summary-level marker-state context were treated as distinct readouts. Matching public raw whole-blood FCS files were available for all

COMBAT paired analysis provided the most direct paired public-data bridge in this study. The paired dataset contained 129 matched biological RNA-CyTOF participant-timepoint rows, including 40 sepsis-only rows from 34 par

The validation strategy reflects the inherent constraints of public-data computational research. Frontiers-relevant validation is addressed through cross-cohort recurrence, continuous-spectrum analysis, broad-cell compos

main_tables.context: Seven public sepsis transcriptomic cohorts were analyzed from processed matrices and harmonized metadata: GSE154918, GSE134347, GSE65682, GSE32707, GSE69063, GSE28750, and GSE54514. Collectively, these datasets contribut

The six-gene host-response panel included RETN, MCEMP1, CYP1B1, S100A12, S100A8, and HK3 and was analyzed as a prespecified host-response panel, not as a newly optimized classifier in this manuscript [19]. The MHC-II/CD7

Across seven independent public bulk transcriptomic cohorts, inflammatory and immunometabolic programs were consistently directionally increased in sepsis, whereas MHC-II/CD74, HLA-DR, and adaptive/T-cell context program

Gene-level ranked enrichment analysis across six recoverable bulk cohorts linked higher RNA decoupling gradient to neutrophil degranulation, IL-1/interleukin, vesicle-transport and protein-secretion context, whereas lowe

Independent paired-dataset discovery did not identify a fully public, mappable non-COMBAT sepsis dataset containing paired whole-blood RNA plus flow/CyTOF or monocyte HLA-DR protein measurements suitable for repeating th

In the sepsis-only paired subset, Death28 involved 11 events among 40 rows. Median MHC-II/CD74 score was lower in Death28-positive rows than in survivors (-1.401 versus -0.834; difference -0.567 SD units; Spearman ρ with

Several important limitations remain. Public cohorts differ in platform, sampling time, disease definition, preprocessing pipeline, and control composition. The RNA results rely on within-cohort scoring, rank/correlation

Code, derived aggregate tables, figure files, and reproducibility metadata supporting this study are available at Zenodo: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20682074. Public source datasets and accessions are listed in Suppl
